issues.title: MCP Server - Method not found: resources/list
Make is easier to select either openai or azure openai, ideally heuristically

comments.body: I will look into this as part of a larger issue for creating many instances of many chat models.  That said, I'm not that keen to ensure we use the same environment variables as that in other ecosystems.

> That said, I'm not that keen to ensure we use the same environment variables as that in other ecosystems.

ok mainly if it is at least the same cardinality and shape will be helpful. For example mechanically rewriting openai ENV to spring's conventions. Thanks for considering @markpollack!

There likely isn't a single standard in python land for all the env-vars that can be translated to spring's property format.  It isn't a path I really want to pursue.

Maybe i'm just tired, but can you explain a bit more clearly what behavior you are looking for

`Specifically, if AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY is non empty, don't export a bean for azure,`

this seems counter intuitive to me.

There are projects such as https://github.com/pivotal-cf/java-cfenv/tree/main/java-cfenv-jdbc that do some conversion in a perhaps more reusable manner.

I need only one line to swap azure for normal openai in python and the `AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY` is reliable as it is canonical.
```python
    openai_client = AsyncAzureOpenAI() if os.getenv("AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY") else None
    model = OpenAIProvider(openai_client=openai_client, use_responses=False).get_model(model_name)
```

I would be easy to heuristically configure azure openai vs normal openai depending on which ENV are defined. I would like to have both azure and openai in the same dependency as sometimes the same app needs to switch between one or the other depending on dev vs prod. Ideal case is auto-configuration using canonical ENV variables defined by openai.

If not keen, please close the issue, I mainly raised this to give a chance for it to be addressed.

issues.title: MCP SSE Server Error - Failed to Send Message Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null
issues.body:
**Bug description**

I have created a MCP Server and MCP client and I have used MCP MVC dependencies to create the Server and client.

Error Log : 

This error stops the MCP server to respond to the MCP Client app.
```
2025-04-11T17:01:05.481-05:00 ERROR 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Failed to send message to session 54411e4e-f5a8-40e6-ae1e-bf81a9edb47b: Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null
```

**Complete stack trace :**

```
2025-04-11T16:59:23.453-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-1] com.llm.filter.SseEndpointFilter         : SSE connection request from: 127.0.0.1, URI: /sse
2025-04-11T16:59:23.465-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-1] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Creating new SSE connection for session: 54411e4e-f5a8-40e6-ae1e-bf81a9edb47b
2025-04-11T16:59:23.470-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-1] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Session 54411e4e-f5a8-40e6-ae1e-bf81a9edb47b initialized with SSE emitter
2025-04-11T16:59:23.561-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-2] com.llm.filter.SseEndpointFilter         : SSE connection request from: 127.0.0.1, URI: /mcp/message
2025-04-11T16:59:23.580-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-2] i.m.server.McpAsyncServer                : Client initialize request - Protocol: 2024-11-05, Capabilities: ClientCapabilities[experimental=null, roots=null, sampling=null], Info: Implementation[name=spring-ai-mcp-client, version=1.0.0]
2025-04-11T16:59:23.618-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-2] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Attempting to broadcast message to 1 active sessions
2025-04-11T16:59:23.654-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-3] com.llm.filter.SseEndpointFilter         : SSE connection request from: 127.0.0.1, URI: /mcp/message
2025-04-11T16:59:23.661-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-4] com.llm.filter.SseEndpointFilter         : SSE connection request from: 127.0.0.1, URI: /mcp/message
2025-04-11T16:59:23.671-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-4] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Attempting to broadcast message to 1 active sessions
2025-04-11T16:59:23.752-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-5] com.llm.filter.SseEndpointFilter         : SSE connection request from: 127.0.0.1, URI: /mcp/message
2025-04-11T16:59:23.752-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-5] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Attempting to broadcast message to 1 active sessions
2025-04-11T16:59:23.826-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-6] com.llm.filter.SseEndpointFilter         : SSE connection request from: 127.0.0.1, URI: /mcp/message
2025-04-11T16:59:23.827-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-6] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Attempting to broadcast message to 1 active sessions
2025-04-11T16:59:54.193-05:00  WARN 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-8] .w.s.m.s.DefaultHandlerExceptionResolver : Ignoring exception, response committed already: org.springframework.web.context.request.async.AsyncRequestTimeoutException
2025-04-11T16:59:54.193-05:00  WARN 79846 --- [nio-8085-exec-8] .w.s.m.s.DefaultHandlerExceptionResolver : Resolved [org.springframework.web.context.request.async.AsyncRequestTimeoutException]
2025-04-11T17:01:05.420-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [io-8085-exec-10] com.llm.filter.SseEndpointFilter         : SSE connection request from: 127.0.0.1, URI: /mcp/message
2025-04-11T17:01:05.431-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] o.s.ai.tool.method.MethodToolCallback    : Starting execution of tool: getPersonsByNationality
2025-04-11T17:01:05.432-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] com.llm.PersonController                 : getPersonsByNationality Nationality param : French 
2025-04-11T17:01:05.465-05:00  INFO 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] com.llm.PersonController                 : Tool response : [] 
2025-04-11T17:01:05.465-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] o.s.ai.tool.method.MethodToolCallback    : Successful execution of tool: getPersonsByNationality
2025-04-11T17:01:05.465-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] o.s.a.t.e.DefaultToolCallResultConverter : Converting tool result to JSON.
2025-04-11T17:01:05.481-05:00 DEBUG 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Attempting to broadcast message to 1 active sessions
2025-04-11T17:01:05.481-05:00 ERROR 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Failed to send message to session 54411e4e-f5a8-40e6-ae1e-bf81a9edb47b: Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null
```


**Environment**

**Spring AI Version**     

```
extra["springAiVersion"] = "1.0.0-M6"
```

**Java Version  :**  Java 21


**Steps to reproduce**

1. Launch the [MCPServer](https://github.com/dilipsundarraj1/spring-mcp/blob/main/mcp-webmvc/mcp-server/src/main/kotlin/com/llm/MCPServer.kt) class 
 2. In the [MCPClient](https://github.com/dilipsundarraj1/spring-mcp/blob/main/mcp-webmvc/mcp-client/src/main/kotlin/com/llm/MCPClient.kt) 
 3. Pass the OPENAI_KEY=<KEY> as an Environmental Variable.
4. Launch the [MCPClient](https://github.com/dilipsundarraj1/spring-mcp/blob/main/mcp-webmvc/mcp-client/src/main/kotlin/com/llm/MCPClient.kt) class.
5. Hit this curl command -> 
6. Launch the `curl -X GET "http://localhost:8086/persons/nationality/France" -H "Content-Type: application/json"`
7. The above request actually triggers a tool call from the MCPClient to the MCPServer app.
8. The **curl** should return a response. This success call happens one or twice , once I notice this error in the **MCPServer** app,  all the calls from the **MCPClient** to Server fails.

```
2025-04-11T17:01:05.481-05:00 ERROR 79846 --- [oundedElastic-1] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Failed to send message to session 54411e4e-f5a8-40e6-ae1e-bf81a9edb47b: Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null
```
9. Am I missing anything basic here ?


**Expected behavior**
- If there is a **Tool Call** for the user prompt then the call to the MCP Server should succeed and it should return a response. 

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**

Here is how I am injecting the MCP tools into the MCP client app

[PersonController](https://github.com/dilipsundarraj1/spring-mcp/blob/6154f67208937b986e8a7d3c4b12692ee712bce0/mcp-webmvc/mcp-client/src/main/kotlin/com/llm/controller/PersonController.kt#L29)
```
@RestController
@RequestMapping("/persons")
class PersonController(
    chatClientBuilder: ChatClient.Builder,
    tools: ToolCallbackProvider,
    mcpClients: List<McpSyncClient>
) {

    private val chatClient: ChatClient = chatClientBuilder
//        .defaultTools(tools)
        .defaultTools( SyncMcpToolCallbackProvider(mcpClients))
        .build()

```

Complete code reference :
 - [MVC Client and Server App](https://github.com/dilipsundarraj1/spring-mcp/blob/main/mcp-webmvc) 

Any help is much appreciated.

comments.body:
Not seeing this issue after upgrading to     `extra["springAiVersion"] = "1.0.0-M7"` .

So closing this issue.
